hypothesis: If adaptive fragment-based and combined diversity constraints are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet-fission molecule generation with NaviDiv, then the generated libraries will show increased chemical diversity—manifested as lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, higher unique fragment percentages, and fewer overrepresented patterns—compared to unconstrained runs while still yielding molecules that satisfy the ΔE_SF criterion, because dynamic penalization prevents mode collapse and the repetition of dominant chemotypes.
